title: Johnny Isakson and David P. Roe, M.D. Veterans Health Care and Benefits Improvement Act of 2020
impact: Modified the 48-month rule. Under Section 1007, past usage of Chapter 33 Post-9/11 GI Bill does not count against the 48 months of Chapter 31 VR&E benefits, enabling veterans to access up to 48 months of VR&E even if they already exhausted their GI Bill.
details: This law dramatically expanded veteran training pathways by separating GI Bill usage from the active Chapter 31 entitlement calculation.

title: Harry W. Colmery Veterans Educational Assistance Act of 2017 (Forever GI Bill)
impact: Removed the 15-year delimiting date for Post-9/11 GI Bill eligibility for veterans discharged on or after January 1, 2013, which influenced Chapter 31 administrative reviews of 12-year basic eligibility windows.
details: Established that veterans with newer discharges have unlimited time to utilize educational benefits, leading to changes in how VA evaluates basic eligibility duration rules.

title: Post-9/11 Veterans Educational Assistance Act of 2008
impact: Established the Post-9/11 GI Bill (Chapter 33) and introduced the election option allowing Chapter 31 participants who have remaining Chapter 33 entitlement to elect the Post-9/11 subsistence allowance rate.
details: Allowed Chapter 31 veterans to receive the generally higher housing allowance (MHA) rate of Chapter 33, making VR&E economically viable for many students.
